title: ssDNA Extraktion
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"19d73","text":"ssDNA Extraction Protocol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5pvs6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fk1bg","text":"This protocol is based on the Oligonucleotide extraction protocol of the NEB PCR \u0026 Clean-up Kit and the Qiagen DNeasy Protocol with a few modifications to maximize DNA output. First results of this protocol will be published soon, including first sequencing results of the so extracted DNA. Please understand that this protocol is so far only tested for tissue (legs and abdomen) of invertebrates (Lepidoptera, genus Hyles). I recommend testing this protocol with tissue of non valuable specimens first. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":417,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7s1hk","text":"Make sure to not touch the specimen/tissue at any given point, always wear gloves and clean all used forecepts before and after usage with DNAaway or similar.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":63,"length":18},{"style":"bold","offset":86,"length":47}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bg3k5","text":"Make sure to clean the sample if possible before you start the Lysis (by UV-light or chlor, not possible in insect legs or soft tissue).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e425","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"82ah8","text":"Only use this Extraction protocol in a cleaned environment, idealy in an aDNA-laboratory.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b4ebp","text":"Take full pre-cautions;","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b2s27","text":"clean your whole bench before DNA extraction","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b76gb","text":"always clean your gloves by using DNAaway after touching the tube of a sample","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"l2g8","text":"take care of aerosols! do not touch/open any tube while your gloves are still wet","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"es5sc","text":"of cause only use filter-tips","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"b1j73","text":"Input amount of DNA to be purified should not exceed the binding  capacity of the column (5 μg). A starting sample volume of 50 μl is  recommended. For smaller samples, nuclease-free water can be used to  adjust the volume to the recommended volume range. Centrifugation should  be carried out at 16,000 x g in a standard laboratory microcentrifuge at room temperature.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":306,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9do0n","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"703m3","text":"Buffer Preparation:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"82b0c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"81vng","text":"Add ethanol to Monarch DNA Wash Buffer prior to use (4 volumes of ≥ 95% ethanol per volume of Monarch DNA Wash Buffer). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"859oh","text":" For 50-prep kit add 20 ml of ethanol to 5 ml of Monarch DNA Wash Buffer","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9g1nj","text":"For 250-prep kit add 100 ml of ethanol to 25 ml of Monarch DNA Wash Buffer","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dg6sq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"44crn","text":"Always keep all buffer bottles tightly closed when not in use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2thde","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4mjv8","text":"All centrifugation steps should be carried out at 16,000 x g. (~13K RPM in a typical microcentrifuge). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":102},{"style":"italic","offset":59,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ob03","text":"This ensures all traces of buffer are eluted at each step. \n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Place dry tissue in 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube and grind it, using an oscillating mill (MM 400, Retsch) for 1 min at a frequency of 25 Hz , using two sterile stainless steel balls with a diameter of 3 mm
Insert to the ground tissue 45 µL, Lysis buffer 5 µL, Proteinase K 50 µL, total volume mix well by handshaking, don´t vortex! and gently spin down the solution.
Put the tube in an Mixing Block (MB-102, Thermocell) 56 °C 08:00:00, at least, better overnight Mix gently, and spin again.
Add 100 µL, DNA Cleanup Binding Buffer to the 50 µL, sample mix by pipetting up and down.
Add 300 µL, ethanol (≥ 95%) Mix well by pipetting up and down or flicking the tube. Do not vortex.
Insert column into collection tube, load sample onto column and close the cap. 00:01:00, Centrifuge then discard flow-through
Re-insert column into collection tube. Add 500 µL, DNA Wash Buffer and 00:01:00, Centrifuge Discard flow-through.
Repeat Step 7. This step is recommended for removal of enzymes that may interfere with downstream applications (e.g., Proteinase K).

Transfer column to a clean 1.5 ml microfuge tube. Use care to ensure that the tip of the column does not come into contact with the flow-through. If in doubt, re-spin for 1 minute to ensure traces of salt and ethanol are not carried over to the next step.
Add 17.5 µL, Elution Buffer to the center of the matrix Wait for 00:10:00, Incubation then spin for 00:01:00, Centrifuge to elute the DNA.
Repeat Step 10 to extract all DNA
creator: Franziska Patzold
